opportunities.title: $500 Doesn't Buy You Better Initiative: Beadle & Grimm's Deadfall Set vs. the Rules
opportunities.why_now: The just-announced $500 Deadfall boxed set is a lightning rod for 'prestige vs. substance' debate among GMs.
opportunities.angle: Wry mechanical takedown — use the premium minis/posters as a springboard for a ruling on how (or if) fancy props ever override RAW resolution at the table.

opportunities.title: Send In The Clowns: Running a Krenshar (or a Jester Cultist) as a Horror-Comedy Monster
opportunities.why_now: EN World's clown-themed column is fresh and gives a built-in hook for a creature-tactic short on unsettling, humor-adjacent monsters.
opportunities.angle: Creature tactic breakdown — pick one 5e/Shadowdark monster that reads 'funny' but plays lethal, show the actual stat block trick.

opportunities.title: Mike Shea's Lazy Prep, Stress-Tested Against a DCC Funnel
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish just dropped a fresh 'Lazy Campaigns' prep breakdown right as Mike Shea guested on the big D&D news podcast, making this a hot cross-reference moment.
opportunities.angle: Direct-response short: take one Lazy DM prep step and show whether it survives DCC's brutal funnel structure — dry commentary on where 'lazy' still needs homework.

opportunities.title: What Mike Shea Actually Said About D&D's Future (And Why Your Prep Doesn't Need to Change)
opportunities.why_now: Podcast #405 just aired with a 'ton of D&D news' and Mike Shea as guest — audience is primed for takeaways without watching 2+ hours.
opportunities.angle: Rapid-fire ruling/news digest format — 3 bullet takeaways delivered dry and skeptical, focused only on table-impact, ignoring corporate fluff.

opportunities.title: Posters and Minis Won't Save a Bad Encounter: The Theater-of-the-Mind Ruling Beadle & Grimm's Can't Sell You
opportunities.why_now: The Deadfall set's marketing leans hard on physical props, making it a perfect foil for a ruling on when maps/minis actually matter mechanically vs. when they're just set dressing.
opportunities.angle: Rules-ruling short: define the exact moment (opportunity attacks, AoE, cover) where a map is mechanically required versus purely aesthetic — needle the premium-set hype.
